diff --git a/loangen-agent/agent/core/logging_setup.py b/loangen-agent/agent/core/logging_setup.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..24545c2
--- /dev/null
+++ b/loangen-agent/agent/core/logging_setup.py
@@ -0,0 +1,109 @@
+"""Shared logging helpers for the API server."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import asyncio
+import copy
+import logging
+import re
+import sys
+from typing import Any
+
+# High-frequency call workspace polling — log errors only, never success.
+_QUIET_POLL_PATHS = (
+    "/api/v1/services/calls/presence/heartbeat",
+    "/api/v1/services/calls/workspace",
+    "/api/v1/services/calls/voice-client-token",
+)
+
+_SUCCESS_STATUS_RE = re.compile(r'HTTP/1\.\d"\s+([23]\d{2})\b')
+_PATCHED_UVICORN_SETUP = False
+
+
+class QuietPollingAccessLogFilter(logging.Filter):
+    """Drop successful access logs for high-frequency call workspace polling."""
+
+    def filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool:
+        message = record.getMessage()
+        if not any(path in message for path in _QUIET_POLL_PATHS):
+            return True
+        match = _SUCCESS_STATUS_RE.search(message)
+        if match is None:
+            return True
+        # Suppress 2xx/3xx; keep 4xx/5xx.
+        return int(match.group(1)) >= 400
+
+
+def install_quiet_polling_access_log_filter() -> None:
+    """Attach the polling filter to uvicorn access logs (safe to call repeatedly)."""
+    access_logger = logging.getLogger("uvicorn.access")
+    access_logger.filters = [
+        f for f in access_logger.filters if not isinstance(f, QuietPollingAccessLogFilter)
+    ]
+    access_logger.addFilter(QuietPollingAccessLogFilter())
+
+
+def patch_uvicorn_logging_setup() -> None:
+    """Re-apply the access filter after uvicorn configures logging."""
+    global _PATCHED_UVICORN_SETUP
+    if _PATCHED_UVICORN_SETUP:
+        return
+
+    import uvicorn.config
+
+    method_name = "configure_logging"
+    if not hasattr(uvicorn.config.Config, method_name):
+        # Older uvicorn releases used setup_logging.
+        method_name = "setup_logging"
+    if not hasattr(uvicorn.config.Config, method_name):
+        return
+
+    original_setup = getattr(uvicorn.config.Config, method_name)
+
+    def patched_setup(self: Any) -> None:
+        original_setup(self)
+        install_quiet_polling_access_log_filter()
+
+    setattr(uvicorn.config.Config, method_name, patched_setup)
+    _PATCHED_UVICORN_SETUP = True
+
+
+def get_uvicorn_log_config() -> dict[str, Any]:
+    """Uvicorn logging config with quiet polling on access logs."""
+    from uvicorn.config import LOGGING_CONFIG
+
+    config = copy.deepcopy(LOGGING_CONFIG)
+    config.setdefault("filters", {})
+    config["filters"]["quiet_polling"] = {
+        "()": "agent.core.logging_setup.QuietPollingAccessLogFilter",
+    }
+    access_logger = config.setdefault("loggers", {}).setdefault("uvicorn.access", {})
+    access_logger["filters"] = ["quiet_polling"]
+    return config
+
+
+def _is_windows_client_disconnect(exc: BaseException | None) -> bool:
+    if exc is None:
+        return False
+    if isinstance(exc, ConnectionResetError):
+        return True
+    return isinstance(exc, OSError) and getattr(exc, "winerror", None) == 10054
+
+
+def install_windows_asyncio_exception_handler() -> None:
+    """Suppress noisy Windows proactor disconnect errors from asyncio."""
+    if sys.platform != "win32":
+        return
+
+    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
+    previous_handler = loop.get_exception_handler()
+
+    def handler(inner_loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop, context: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
+        if _is_windows_client_disconnect(context.get("exception")):
+            return
+        if previous_handler is not None:
+            previous_handler(inner_loop, context)
+        else:
+            inner_loop.default_exception_handler(context)
+
+    loop.set_exception_handler(handler)

+async def pick_inbound_target_session(
+    lender_id: str,
+    *,
+    preferred_session_id: Optional[str] = None,
+) -> Optional[str]:
+    """
+    First browser session that is voice-registered and available for inbound Client dial.
+
+    Prefers the session that placed the last outbound call when it is registered and online.
+    """
     online = await presence_mod.list_online_sessions(lender_id)
     available: list[str] = []
     for session_id, data in online:
+        if not data.get("voice_registered"):
+            continue
         status = data.get("status")
         if status in (
             AgentPresenceStatus.AVAILABLE.value,
             AgentPresenceStatus.ON_AI_ONLY.value,
         ):
             available.append(session_id)
+
+    preferred = (preferred_session_id or "").strip()
+    if preferred and preferred in available:
+        return preferred
     return available[0] if available else None

diff --git a/loangen-agent/tests/test_call_messaging.py b/loangen-agent/tests/test_call_messaging.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..5b5e044
--- /dev/null
+++ b/loangen-agent/tests/test_call_messaging.py
@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@
+"""Tests that call-availability errors shown to end users don't leak server config.
+
+When a call cannot be placed (calling not enabled on the account, or an AI call
+to a non-US number), the borrower/lender in the CRM should see a plain, actionable
+message — never the names of internal environment variables, server settings, or
+the voice vendor.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import unittest
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch
+
+from fastapi import HTTPException
+
+from agent.core.config import settings as app_settings
+from agent.services.calls.service import get_voice_client_token
+from agent.services.smbcontacts.service import initiate_ai_call, initiate_direct_call
+
+
+def _error_message(exc: HTTPException) -> str:
+    detail = exc.detail
+    if isinstance(detail, dict):
+        return str(detail.get("message", ""))
+    return str(detail)
+
+
+class UserFacingCallErrorTests(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase):
+    async def test_ai_call_rejection_hides_internal_config(self) -> None:
+        contact = MagicMock()
+        with patch(
+            "agent.services.smbcontacts.service._get_lender_contact",
+            new_callable=AsyncMock,
+            return_value=contact,
+        ), patch(
+            "agent.services.smbcontacts.service._normalize_contact_phone",
+            new_callable=AsyncMock,
+            return_value="+447911123456",
+        ), patch.object(app_settings, "cartesia_outbound_us_only", True):
+            with self.assertRaises(HTTPException) as ctx:
+                await initiate_ai_call(
+                    contact_id="c1",
+                    lender_id="l1",
+                    lender_business_name="Acme",
+                    session_id="s1",
+                )
+
+        message = _error_message(ctx.exception)
+        self.assertTrue(message.strip())
+        self.assertNotIn("Cartesia", message)
+        self.assertNotIn("CARTESIA_OUTBOUND_US_ONLY", message)
+        self.assertNotIn("+447911123456", message)
+
+    async def test_direct_call_rejection_hides_internal_config(self) -> None:
+        with patch(
+            "agent.services.smbcontacts.service.direct_call_configured",
+            return_value=False,
+        ):
+            with self.assertRaises(HTTPException) as ctx:
+                await initiate_direct_call(
+                    contact_id="c1",
+                    lender_id="l1",
+                    lender_business_name="Acme",
+                    session_id="s1",
+                )
+
+        message = _error_message(ctx.exception)
+        self.assertTrue(message.strip())
+        self.assertNotIn("CALLS_DIRECT_ENABLED", message)
+        self.assertNotIn("PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL", message)
+        self.assertNotIn("TWILIO_OUTBOUND_CALLER_ID", message)
+
+    async def test_voice_token_outage_message_is_user_facing(self) -> None:
+        with patch(
+            "agent.services.calls.service.direct_calls_enabled",
+            return_value=False,
+        ):
+            with self.assertRaises(HTTPException) as ctx:
+                await get_voice_client_token(lender_id="l1", session_id="s1")
+
+        message = _error_message(ctx.exception)
+        self.assertTrue(message.strip())
+        self.assertNotIn("server", message.lower())
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

+class PickInboundTargetSessionTests(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase):
+    async def test_skips_sessions_that_cannot_receive_voice(self) -> None:
+        with patch(
+            "agent.services.calls.inbound.presence_mod.list_online_sessions",
+            new_callable=AsyncMock,
+            return_value=[
+                ("sess_a", {"status": "available", "voice_registered": False}),
+                ("sess_b", {"status": "available", "voice_registered": True}),
+            ],
+        ):
+            target = await pick_inbound_target_session("lender_a")
+        self.assertEqual(target, "sess_b")
+
+    async def test_no_target_when_no_session_can_receive_voice(self) -> None:
+        with patch(
+            "agent.services.calls.inbound.presence_mod.list_online_sessions",
+            new_callable=AsyncMock,
+            return_value=[
+                ("sess_a", {"status": "available", "voice_registered": False}),
+                ("sess_b", {"status": "available", "voice_registered": False}),
+            ],
+        ):
+            target = await pick_inbound_target_session("lender_a")
+        self.assertIsNone(target)
+
+

+    last_outbound = MagicMock()

+    last_outbound.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+    last_outbound.initiated_by_session_id = initiator_session_id
+    return last_outbound
+
+

diff --git a/loangen-agent/tests/test_logging_setup.py b/loangen-agent/tests/test_logging_setup.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f89df7d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/loangen-agent/tests/test_logging_setup.py
@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@
+"""Tests for API access-log noise suppression on high-frequency call polling.
+
+The call workspace polls a few endpoints several times a second. Their
+successful (2xx/3xx) access-log lines must be suppressed, while client/server
+errors (4xx/5xx) on the same endpoints — and every other route — must still be
+logged.
+"""
+
+import logging
+import unittest
+
+
+def _access_record(message: str) -> logging.LogRecord:
+    return logging.LogRecord(
+        name="uvicorn.access",
+        level=logging.INFO,
+        pathname="",
+        lineno=0,
+        msg=message,
+        args=(),
+        exc_info=None,
+    )
+
+
+class QuietPollingFilterTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_suppresses_successful_polling(self) -> None:
+        from agent.core.logging_setup import QuietPollingAccessLogFilter
+
+        filt = QuietPollingAccessLogFilter()
+        heartbeat = (
+            '127.0.0.1:53125 - "POST /api/v1/services/calls/presence/heartbeat HTTP/1.1" 200 OK'
+        )
+        workspace = '127.0.0.1:53125 - "GET /api/v1/services/calls/workspace HTTP/1.1" 200 OK'
+        token = '127.0.0.1:53125 - "GET /api/v1/services/calls/voice-client-token HTTP/1.1" 200 OK'
+        self.assertFalse(filt.filter(_access_record(heartbeat)))
+        self.assertFalse(filt.filter(_access_record(workspace)))
+        self.assertFalse(filt.filter(_access_record(token)))
+
+    def test_keeps_polling_errors(self) -> None:
+        from agent.core.logging_setup import QuietPollingAccessLogFilter
+
+        filt = QuietPollingAccessLogFilter()
+        failed = (
+            '127.0.0.1:53125 - "GET /api/v1/services/calls/workspace HTTP/1.1" 500 Internal Server Error'
+        )
+        unauthorized = (
+            '127.0.0.1:53125 - "POST /api/v1/services/calls/presence/heartbeat HTTP/1.1" 401 Unauthorized'
+        )
+        self.assertTrue(filt.filter(_access_record(failed)))
+        self.assertTrue(filt.filter(_access_record(unauthorized)))
+
+    def test_keeps_other_routes(self) -> None:
+        from agent.core.logging_setup import QuietPollingAccessLogFilter
+
+        filt = QuietPollingAccessLogFilter()
+        contacts = '127.0.0.1:53125 - "GET /api/v1/services/smb-contacts?page=1 HTTP/1.1" 200 OK'
+        self.assertTrue(filt.filter(_access_record(contacts)))
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
diff --git a/loangen-agent/tests/test_presence.py b/loangen-agent/tests/test_presence.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..47ef621
--- /dev/null
+++ b/loangen-agent/tests/test_presence.py
@@ -0,0 +1,100 @@
+"""Tests for voice-registration persistence in agent presence records.
+
+A browser tab reports (on its heartbeat) whether it has registered with the
+voice SDK and can therefore receive an inbound Client dial. That signal must be
+stored on the tab's presence record and must survive presence updates that do
+not carry it (e.g. a plain status heartbeat, or a call-state change), otherwise
+the tab silently stops receiving return calls.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import unittest
+from unittest.mock import patch
+
+from agent.services.calls.enums import AgentPresenceStatus
+from agent.services.calls.presence import get_presence, set_presence
+
+
+class _FakeRedis:
+    """Minimal in-memory async stand-in for the presence redis client."""
+
+    def __init__(self) -> None:
+        self._store: dict[str, str] = {}
+
+    async def set(self, key: str, value: str, ex: int | None = None) -> None:
+        self._store[key] = value
+
+    async def get(self, key: str) -> str | None:
+        return self._store.get(key)
+
+    async def delete(self, key: str) -> None:
+        self._store.pop(key, None)
+
+    async def scan_iter(self, match: str | None = None, count: int | None = None):
+        for key in list(self._store):
+            yield key
+
+
+class VoiceRegistrationPresenceTests(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase):
+    def setUp(self) -> None:
+        self.redis = _FakeRedis()
+        patcher = patch(
+            "agent.services.calls.presence.get_redis_client",
+            return_value=self.redis,
+        )
+        patcher.start()
+        self.addCleanup(patcher.stop)
+
+    async def test_presence_records_voice_registration(self) -> None:
+        await set_presence(
+            lender_id="l1",
+            session_id="s1",
+            status=AgentPresenceStatus.AVAILABLE,
+            voice_registered=True,
+        )
+        data = await get_presence("l1", "s1")
+        self.assertIsNotNone(data)
+        assert data is not None
+        self.assertTrue(data.get("voice_registered"))
+
+    async def test_status_only_update_preserves_voice_registration(self) -> None:
+        await set_presence(
+            lender_id="l1",
+            session_id="s1",
+            status=AgentPresenceStatus.AVAILABLE,
+            voice_registered=True,
+        )
+        # A later presence update that does not carry the voice-registration flag
+        # (a plain status heartbeat, or a call-state transition) must not drop it.
+        await set_presence(
+            lender_id="l1",
+            session_id="s1",
+            status=AgentPresenceStatus.ON_HUMAN_CALL,
+        )
+        data = await get_presence("l1", "s1")
+        self.assertIsNotNone(data)
+        assert data is not None
+        self.assertTrue(data.get("voice_registered"))
+
+    async def test_explicit_deregistration_clears_voice_registration(self) -> None:
+        await set_presence(
+            lender_id="l1",
+            session_id="s1",
+            status=AgentPresenceStatus.AVAILABLE,
+            voice_registered=True,
+        )
+        await set_presence(
+            lender_id="l1",
+            session_id="s1",
+            status=AgentPresenceStatus.AVAILABLE,
+            voice_registered=False,
+        )
+        data = await get_presence("l1", "s1")
+        self.assertIsNotNone(data)
+        assert data is not None
+        self.assertFalse(data.get("voice_registered"))
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
